description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

aliases: Richard T. Crane Medical Prep High School
description: public 4-year medical prep high school on the Near West Side of Chicago, Illinois, United States
description_wiki: Richard T. Crane Medical Prep High School (formerly known as Crane Tech Prep or Crane Tech High School) is a public four-year medical prep high school located in the Near West Side neighborhood of Chicago, Illinois, United States.

description: city in and county seat of Milwaukee County, and largest city in State of Wisconsin, United States
description_wiki: Milwaukee is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Wisconsin.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

description: artillery piece designed to deploy with army units in the field
description_wiki: Field artillery is a category of mobile artillery used to support armies in the field.

description: United States Armed Forces Training Station
description_wiki: Fort Sill is a major United States Army post north of Lawton, Oklahoma, approximately 85 miles (137 km) southwest of Oklahoma City, established in 1869 during the Indian Wars.

description: communication code with signals representing letters, punctuation marks and numbers
description_wiki: Morse code is a telecommunications method which encodes text characters as standardized sequences of two different signal durations, called dots and dashes, or dits and dahs.

description: military police in the United States Army
description_wiki: The United States Army Military Police Corps (USAMPC) is the uniformed law enforcement branch of the United States Army.

description: military rank held by junior enlisted personnel in some countries' armed forces
description_wiki: Private first class (French: Soldat de 1re classe; Spanish: Soldado de primera) is a military rank held by junior enlisted personnel in many armed forces.
